title: Add basic WYSIWYG heading controls (H1/H2) to Essay editor

requirements.text: Extend the Essay rich-text model to support heading levels (at minimum H1 and H2) so that an essay block can be rendered/stored distinctly as an H1 vs H2 heading.

quotes: ok ad some basic wysiwyg functionality for the Essays. I want to be able to make things bold, italic. h1/h2 etc.

acceptanceCriteria: Backend RichText heading blocks can represent at least two levels (H1 and H2) without losing information on save/load.
Existing persisted essays/pages that contain heading blocks remain readable after upgrade and default to a sensible heading level (e.g., H2 or H1) when level is missing.
Frontend types generated/consumed from the backend reflect the new heading-level capability (no TypeScript type errors).

requirements.text: Update the admin Essay editor toolbar to provide WYSIWYG controls for H1 and H2 headings in addition to the existing Bold/Italic controls, allowing the user to convert the current paragraph (or selected block) into an H1 or H2 heading and back to a paragraph.

quotes: I want to be able to make things bold, italic. h1/h2 etc.

acceptanceCriteria: Essay editor UI includes explicit H1 and H2 controls alongside the existing formatting buttons.
Using H1/H2 controls updates the internal RichText representation used when saving (not just a visual preview).
Toggling heading level off returns the block to a normal paragraph.
No regression to existing bold/italic behavior (buttons and Ctrl/Cmd+B / Ctrl/Cmd+I still work as before).

requirements.text: Update RichText rendering to display heading blocks using the correct HTML heading tag and styling based on the stored heading level (H1 renders as <h1>, H2 renders as <h2>).
acceptanceCriteria: Published essays render heading blocks using <h1> for level 1 and <h2> for level 2 (and do not render all headings as the same level).
Heading rendering remains compatible with existing content that has headings created before this change (defaults to a consistent level).

requirements.text: Update Markdown export to emit the correct number of leading '#' characters for headings based on heading level (H1 -> '#', H2 -> '##').
acceptanceCriteria: Exported markdown uses '# ' for H1 headings and '## ' for H2 headings.
Exported markdown for essays created before this change remains valid and uses a sensible default level for headings without an explicit level.

requirements.text: Add a state migration for existing canister data if required by the heading-level schema change, ensuring upgrade does not trap and existing essays/pages remain accessible.
acceptanceCriteria: Canister upgrade succeeds without trapping due to type/schema mismatch.
Existing stored RichText heading blocks are migrated or interpreted with a default heading level so they continue to render and export correctly.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo (no additional canisters/services).
Do not edit files under frontend immutablePaths (compose existing UI components instead).
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Implement a full-featured rich-text editor framework (e.g., ProseMirror/Slate/Tiptap) with complex behaviors like nested lists, tables, mentions, or collaborative editing.
Add new authentication methods beyond Internet Identity.
